items.title: 'People assume we're not real': MPs open up about mental health challenges

children.content: A national study has found 70 per cent of politicians feel their job is affecting their mental health. The ABC spoke to three Tasmanian MPs, including the state's premier, about their experiences.

articles.title: TassieCast: Do we care enough about our pollies' mental health?

children.content: In what's believed to be the biggest study of its kind, a staggering 70 per cent of almost 300 of our federal, state and local politicians who took part say the job has adversely affected their mental health.

image.altText: Man sitting in garden looking stressed

gallery.altText: A composite image using headshots of Ella Haddad, Jeremy Rockliff and Ruth Forrest.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image using headshots of Ella Haddad, Jeremy Rockliff and Ruth Forrest.

items.title: Study finds decades of 'intensive' logging worsens bushfire risk

children.content: Scientists have studied satellite images of a Tasmanian bushfire and found regrowth from extensive logging and recent bushfires has "absolutely" increased the risk of more severe bushfires.

image.altText: Forest lit up by unseen bushfire flames.

items.title: Call for 'collective action' to address gendered violence in Tasmania

children.content: A new platform, dubbed Justice For Her, is centred on making small, actionable pledges to help people overcome the overwhelming statistics on gendered violence, which often lead to inaction.

image.altText: Sally Haynes and Carolyn Self launching "Justice for Her" 2026-04-08 10:04:00

items.title: Emperor penguins, Antarctic fur seals now endangered due to climate change

children.content: The iconic emperor penguin and the Antarctic fur seal are edging closer to extinction, according to a projection by the world's largest environmental network.

articles.title: The emperor penguin and Antarctic fur seal are now considered endangered

children.content: The iconic emperor penguin and the Antarctic fur seal are edging closer to extinction, according to a projection by the world's largest environmental network.

image.altText: Eleven black and white penguins slide along ice in a row.

gallery.altText: A penguin chicken stands in the foreground. Camera crews are in the distance.

thumbnail.altText: A penguin chicken stands in the foreground. Camera crews are in the distance.

items.title: 'Made it very attractive': Free rides luring thousands more onto buses

children.content: The temporary ability to catch public transport for free is turning some Tasmanians into "converts", while others are using it for new experiences.

image.altText: A man in a 'PROTECT TAKAYNA' shirt stands in front of a bus going to Devonport.

items.title: Grace Tame Foundation announces closure

children.content: The Grace Tame Foundation, established by the Tasmanian-born survivor of child sexual abuse, has announced it is closing because securing funding is becoming "increasingly challenging".

image.altText: A woman wearing a grey cardigan with her hair in a pony tail looks seriously at the camera in a building corridor.

items.title: Artemis II crew prepares to ride 'fireball' back to Earth

children.content: Here's a breakdown of how the Orion space capsule copes with high temperatures, slows down, and what happens as it splashes down.

The Orion space capsule floats over the ocean, slowed down by balloons.

items.title: Dockers launch late fightback to pip Magpies in frantic finish

children.content: Fremantle wins its fourth match in a row after surviving a tense final few minutes against Collingwood at Adelaide Oval.

image.altText: Jye Amiss flexes his arms during the Dockers' AFL win over the Magpies.

items.title: A historic week in China could shape the Xi-Trump meeting

children.content: Taiwan's opposition leader has done something for the first time in a decade, and experts say it could shape relations between Beijing, Taipei and Washington.

image.altText: Xi Jinping and Cheng Li-wun shake hands

items.title: Commuters trapped for hours in freeway gridlock blast lack of signage

children.content: Commuters stuck queuing for up to four hours following a truck crash on the South Eastern Freeway on Thursday blast the transport department over a lack of signs warning them of the road closure ahead.

image.altText: Cars fill a freeway

items.title: Family devastated after Qld man charged with murdering girlfriend on road trip

children.content: A Sunshine Coast man has been charged with murdering his girlfriend in the Northern Territory. Ahead of his appearance in court, her sister has spoken of her family's heartbreak.

image.altText: Christine Joyce Diane Marshall Hunter

items.title: Deep in a Ugandan forest, chimpanzees wage a lethal 'civil war'

children.content: A decades-long study reveals hundreds of chimpanzees waging a violent and deadly conflict against their former friends in their remote forest home.

image.altText: Looking through a frame of green leaves at one chimpanzee showing its teeth and another chimp watching on.

gallery.altText: a chimpanzee with its mouth open in the forest

thumbnail.altText: a chimpanzee with its mouth open in the forest

items.title: Hegseth's religious rhetoric and Pentagon prayers rile some Americans

children.content: The ABC visits US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's church, whose leaders challenge the legitimacy of democracy and key principles in the US constitution.

image.altText: Pete Hegseth stands and speaks at a podium with a Department of War logo on it.

items.title: Friends, family thank the community for helping to find missing woman

children.content: The family and friends of a woman who was missing for a week in south-western Victoria have thanked the community after she was found safe and well.

image.altText: collage of four photos of a woman with black hair

items.title: Jury hears mum accused of son's murder allegedly said he had 'no life'

children.content: A Gold Coast woman accused of killing her son allegedly called him a "potato" with "no life" and claimed she "wanted to put something in his drink so he will just not be here", a jury heard.

image.altText: A middle aged woman wearing dark sunglasses and a black tshirt.

items.title: 'Your actions are deplorable': Man jailed over police officer head stomp

children.content: Koby Weaver is jailed over his role in what is described as a "brutal" attack which left an off-duty police officer with serious injuries.

image.altText: Koby is a young man who wears a suit jacket and sunglasses and walks on a CBD street.

items.title: Euphoria ushered in a new generation of A-listers. Now, it's finally back

children.content: Sam Levinson's controversial teen series has had to overcome four years of actor deaths, character departures and on-set tension to make it to season three.

articles.title: What to watch and listen to this weekend

children.content: An old favourite, new Courtney Barnett and a tasty Korean vegetarian stew.

items.title: 'Never friends': Melania Trump criticises Epstein 'lies' in unusual address

children.content: In an unusual White House address, Melania Trump denies having a friendship with Jeffrey Epstein and complains about "mean-spirited" attempts to smear her.

avatar.alt: Brad Ryan is pictured in front of a news studio background, which shows a bridge in a city.

items.title: Family 'empty and shocked' after surfer's death over $50 dispute

children.content: Guy Haymes was left in a vegetative state after an assault during a dispute over $50 in February, 2024.

image.altText: A middle-aged man wearing a cap smiles at the camera.

items.title: High Court urged to toss reduced sentence for Emma Lovell killer

children.content: Lawyers for the Queensland government have travelled to Canberra to urge the High Court to toss out a reduced sentence for a teen jailed for killing Emma Lovell in 2022.

image.altText: A photo of a framed picture of a blonde woman hanging on a wall

items.title: Peter Dutton appointed to Queensland investment board

children.content: Mr Dutton's has kept a low public profile since he led the Coalition to defeat at the 2025 federal election, and also lost his seat of Dickson.

image.altText: Peter Dutton speaking to journalists.

items.title: Aussie duo suffer shock upsets as Britain takes control of BJK Cup tie

children.content: Australia's quest to win a place in the Billie Jean King Cup Finals is off to the worst possible start after losses in both opening-day singles matches.

image.altText: Female tennis player stretches out to play a forehand in a match

items.title: Roads destroyed as slow-moving TC Maila batters the Pacific on its way to Qld

children.content: Roads and buildings have been destroyed across multiple Pacific islands as slow-moving Tropical Cyclone Maila heads towards Papua New Guinea, with the eye of the storm set to cross over two remote islands early tomorrow.

articles.title: Warning system made WA community feel 'unsafe' during ex-Cyclone Narelle

children.content: Residents in the Pilbara raise concerns over the state's warning system for cyclones following ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle last month.

items.title: Long queues at EV chargers highlight gaps in charging infrastructure

children.content: Sales of electric vehicles have surged, but NSW government mapping shows regional areas are still lacking EV charging stations.

image.altText: Close up of electric vehicle being charged at a station in regional NSW.

items.title: Life a 'continuous nightmare' for family of woman set alight by her son

children.content: NSW grandmother Nerol Doble was murdered by her son Shannon James Doble in 2024 after an argument over the use of a jerry can. During a sentencing hearing, family members told the court of the hell his actions have left them in.

image.altText: An elderly woman with short grey hair wearing a white and blue striped shirt.

items.title: Iron ore workers stood down with no pay, the company blames the fuel crisis

children.content: A Northern Territory mine has stood down workers and stopped operations, blaming the high price of fuel. Unions say that's "rubbish".

image.altText: A digger is filling up a truck with iron ore in the NT.

items.title: Picklum and Nichols set for all-Australian semi at Bells Beach

children.content: Molly Picklum wants to ring the bell but will need to topple defending champion and countrywoman Isabella Nichols after the pair starred again in the World Surf League's season opener.

image.altText: Female surfer on top of a a wave during a competition

items.title: Snoozing seal blocks road in popular coastal spot

children.content: Locals say they are not surprised the seal, described as a regular visitor, decided to stop for a snooze on the roadway.

items.title: Man pleads guilty to dealing with millions of dollars from illegal tobacco

children.content: Malek Alhmedai's hire car had $4 million stashed inside, with the money linked to illegal tobacco sales.

image.altText: Boxes of cigarettes surrounded by Australian cash.

items.title: Afrika Bambaataa, rap icon accused of sexual abuse, dead at 67

children.content: The Universal Zulu Nation founder was a beloved hip hop icon before sexual abuse and trafficking allegations surfaced in the 2010s.

items.title: Racism erupts after Melbourne announces Little India plans

children.content: Social media posts about the announcement of a Little India precinct in Melbourne are flooded with racist comments, leaving some Indians with mixed feelings about the plan.

items.title: This couple has a fixed price contract to build their home — it's gone up $120k

children.content: Contractors, business owners and economists warn that thousands of construction industry businesses could go bust as a result of supply chain disruptions caused by the Iran War, unless the government intervenes.

items.title: How one of the world's best ballet dancers ended up in Australia

children.content: The Australian Ballet helped put David Hallberg back together after a grave injury. He now returns that favour and leads our national ballet company with a new verve and energy.
